data.title: Erasca GAAP EPS of -$0.11 in-line
data.description:

  • Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities were $362.4 million as of September 30, 2025, compared to $440.5 million as of December 31, 2024.

data.title: Piedmont Realty Trust launches tender offer for 9.25% senior notes due 2028
data.description:
• Piedmont Realty Trust (PDM) announced a cash tender offer by its operating partnership to repurchase any and all of its 9.250% senior notes due 2028.
  • The notes have $532.46M in principal amount outstanding.
  • The offer references the 3.50% U.S. Treasury due November 15, 2028, with a fixed spread of 95 basis points.

data.title: Novartis says novel antimalarial succeeded in late-stage trial
data.description:
Novartis (NVS) (OTCPK:NVSEF) has announced that its next-generation malaria treatment, KLU156 (GanLum), developed with Swiss nonprofit Medicines for Malaria Venture, reached the primary endpoint in a late-stage trial.

Citing data from its Africa-based KALUMA Phase 3 trial, the Swiss drugmaker said on Wednesday that GanLum met the study’s primary endpoint, performing at a similar level (non-inferiority) to the current standard of care.

Data from KALUMA, as presented at the ongoing American Society of Tropical Medicine and Hygiene annual meeting 2025, indicated that the combination therapy led to a PCR-corrected cure rate of 97.4% compared to 94.0% for standard of care.

That equated to cure rates of 99.2% and 96.7%, respectively, the company said, citing a per-protocol analysis.

Novartis (NVS) said it will take steps as soon as possible to seek regulatory approvals for GanLum, which received Fast Track and Orphan Drug Designations from the U.S. FDA in 2022.

The company added that if licensed, the antimalarial could be the first major innovation in malaria medications since the approval of artemisinin-based combination therapies, the current gold standard of care, more than two decades ago.

data.title: Charlie’s Holdings to end hemp/CBD product sales ahead of planned uplist
data.description:
• Charlie’s Holdings (CHUC) will discontinue all hemp/CBD-related product sales and close its Don Polly division.
  • The move is part of preparations for an uplist to a national securities exchange.
  • The decision follows restrictions by exchanges and brokers on companies involved in hemp/CBD businesses, despite their federal legality under the 2018 Farm Bill.
  • The company’s board unanimously approved the resolution to support the uplisting and improve stock liquidity.

data.title: Cisco flirts with new all-time high after Q1 results; analysts point to Networking strength
data.description:
Cisco Systems (CSCO) shares had climbed 6% by early trading on Thursday, propelling it above its all-time closing high of $77.31, which was set more than 25 years ago in March 2020.

Driving the gains was another quarterly financial report that surpassed market expectations. Cisco's first quarter fiscal 2026 earnings report marked the 14th consecutive set of results to surpass the consensus estimates for earnings per share and total revenue. 

Financial analysts posted positive reactions following the results, pointing to strong growth in Cisco's Networking segment, which increased by 15% year over year.

"AI infrastructure orders were $1.3B, a new record, and with Neocloud, Sovereign, Enterprise orders of $200M+ total orders from these new customer categories totaled ~$1.5B, a new record," said KeyBanc analyst Brandon Nispel in an investor note. "CSCO called out it now expects AI Infrastructure orders to be 'at least two times' the orders received from the same set of customers in '25 (>$2.0B), and called out a pipeline of Neocloud/Sovereign of $2.0B."

KeyBanc reiterated its Overweight rating and increased its price target to $87 from $77.

Cisco's Observability and Services revenue segments also increased by 6.2% and 2.1% year over year, respectively, while the Security and Collaboration segments dipped 2% and 3%, respectively.

"CSCO highlighted that it saw a notable shift in terms of how customers consume Splunk in 1Q, where customers moved from on-premise to cloud deals rapidly during the quarter, where cloud deals are recognized ratably and revenue recognition is delayed," Nispel added.

Meanwhile, Piper Sandler retained its Neutral rating but hiked its price target to $86 from $70.

"Cisco continues to put good quarters in a row as the webscale AI infrastructure buildout is now being helped by the campus refresh opportunity showing up early," said Piper Sandler analysts James Fish and Caden Dahl in a note.

Finally, Morgan Stanley retained its Overweight rating on Cisco and increased its price target to $82 from $77. 

"While we had entered the quarter relatively muted, we did note that AI orders greater than $1bn would be a positive catalyst, which Cisco outperformed with $1.3bn of orders," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Mary Lenox in a note. "With the company now expecting ~$3bn of revenue in FY26 from AI, Cisco’s AI business has gone from ~2% of the company to almost 5% in a couple of years, becoming a more meaningful contributor to growth."

Cisco competitors, such as Arista Networks (ANET) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) had inched down 1% and 0.6%, respectively.

data.title: Upexi announces $50M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Upexi (UPXI) said on Thursday that its board has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $50M of its outstanding common stock.
  • The share repurchase program will enhance Upexi’s flexibility to purchase shares of its common stock in the open market, subject to market conditions and other factors. 
  • The company expects to execute share repurchases opportunistically, consistent with its disciplined capital allocation approach. 
  • Upexi (UPXI) shares were up more than 2% in pre-market trading.
